title: Binary Search Function with Incorrect Handling of Edge Cases

statement.markdown: Implement a binary search function that finds an element in a sorted array. The function should return the index of the element if found, -1 otherwise. Handle the edge cases where the array is empty and the target element is at the start or end of the array.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a sorted array of integers and a target integer. The array will contain at least one element and the target element will be either present in the array or not.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the index of the target element if found, -1 otherwise.
statement.notes: The function should use binary search algorithm and handle the edge cases correctly. The array will be sorted in ascending order.

examples.explanation: The target element 3 is present in the array at index 2.
The target element 6 is not present in the array.
The target element 1 is present in the array at index 0.

hints.text: Think about the base cases for the binary search function.
Consider the edge cases where the array is empty and the target element is at the start or end of the array.

editorial.title: Binary Search Function
editorial.intuition: The binary search function uses a divide-and-conquer approach to find the target element in the array. It repeatedly divides the search interval in half and selects the subinterval that contains the target element.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to iterate over the entire array and check each element. However, this would have a time complexity of O(n), which is not efficient for large arrays.
editorial.betterSolution: The binary search function has a time complexity of O(log n), which is much more efficient than the brute force approach.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a binary search algorithm with correct handling of edge cases. It returns the index of the target element if found, -1 otherwise.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the binary search function correctly handles all possible cases, including the base cases and edge cases.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run, we can start with a small array and test the function with different inputs.

functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, arr: list[int], target: int) -> int:
functionSignature.javascript: function binarySearch(arr: number[], target: number): number
